basicDetails.schemeName: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar’s Electric Auto (e-Auto) Subsidy Scheme

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme "Dr. B. R. Ambedkar’s Electric Auto (e-Auto) Subsidy Scheme" was launched by the Adi Dravidar Welfare and Scheduled Tribes Welfare, Government of Puducherry. The scheme aims to socially and economically empower Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe women by supporting them in income-generating activities through the use of electric autos. The scheme provides a subsidy of 75% of the total cost of the electric auto (e-Auto) or ₹3,00,000/- whichever is less, after utilizing Central Government incentives. It promotes eco-friendly public transportation and intends to make these women self-reliant. The applications for this scheme are accepted offline.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - A subsidy of 75% of the total cost of the electric auto (e-Auto) or ₹3,00,000/- whichever is less will be paid. - This subsidy is paid after utilizing Central Government incentives under the Pradhan Mantri E-DRIVE Scheme (FAME-3), which provides a maximum subsidy of ₹50,000/-. - The total cost of the electric auto will be calculated excluding extra accessories. ##### ***Disbursement*** - **In case the beneficiary has already purchased the vehicle:** The applicant must submit the original invoice along with the application to the Department, and the subsidy amount will then be credited to the beneficiary’s account.- **In case the beneficiary has not purchased the vehicle:** The applicant must pay 25% of the total cost of the vehicle as an initial payment to the dealer. The applicant has to submit the invoice for payment along with the application, and the subsidy amount will then be credited to the dealer's account. ##### ***Post-Selection Obligations*** - The beneficiary should not cause or permit a male driver to drive the vehicle. - The beneficiary must retain all relevant documents for 5 years from the date of subsidy disbursement. - The applicant has no power to sale/lease or any other transfer of ownership of the vehicle for a period of 5 years. - If the information furnished by the beneficiary is false or if the electric auto is found to have been sold within the 5-year period, the total subsidy is liable to be returned to the Government or recovered with interest from the beneficiary.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to socially and economically empower Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe women by supporting them in income-generating activities through electric autos. It provides a subsidy up to 75% of the cost (maximum ₹3,00,000/-) for electric autos to Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe women.

children.text: The scheme "Dr. B. R. Ambedkar’s Electric Auto (e-Auto) Subsidy Scheme" was launched by the Adi Dravidar Welfare and Scheduled Tribes Welfare, Government of Puducherry. The scheme aims to socially and economically empower Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe women by supporting them in income-generating activities through the use of electric autos. The scheme provides a subsidy of 75% of the total cost of the electric auto (e-Auto) or ₹3,00,000/- whichever is less, after utilizing Central Government incentives. It promotes eco-friendly public transportation and intends to make these women self-reliant. The applications for this scheme are accepted offline.
A subsidy of 75% of the total cost of the electric auto (e-Auto) or ₹3,00,000/- whichever is less will be paid.
This subsidy is paid after utilizing Central Government incentives under the Pradhan Mantri E-DRIVE Scheme (FAME-3), which provides a maximum subsidy of ₹50,000/-.
The total cost of the electric auto will be calculated excluding extra accessories.
Disbursement
In case the beneficiary has already purchased the vehicle:
The applicant must submit the original invoice along with the application to the Department, and the subsidy amount will then be credited to the beneficiary’s account.
In case the beneficiary has not purchased the vehicle:

The applicant must pay 25% of the total cost of the vehicle as an initial payment to the dealer. The applicant has to submit the invoice for payment along with the application, and the subsidy amount will then be credited to the dealer's account.
Post-Selection Obligations
The beneficiary should not cause or permit a male driver to drive the vehicle.
The beneficiary must retain all relevant documents for 5 years from the date of subsidy disbursement.
The applicant has no power to sale/lease or any other transfer of ownership of the vehicle for a period of 5 years.

If the information furnished by the beneficiary is false or if the electric auto is found to have been sold within the 5-year period, the total subsidy is liable to be returned to the Government or recovered with interest from the beneficiary.
Step 1:
The interested applicant should take a printout of the
prescribed format of the application form
Step 2:
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 3:
The applicants in the Puducherry region should submit the application to the
Director
. The applicants in the Karaikal and Yanam regions should submit the application to the
Assistant Director
. The applicants in the Mahe region should submit the application to the
Regional Administrator
Post-Application Processes
Step 1:
Review and Selection Methodology
All applications must be reviewed by the designated Welfare Inspector, and only those applications deemed eligible will be considered by the Selection Committee.
Selection should be made by giving an advertisement in the leading dailies (one English and one regional language daily, such as Tamil, Telugu, or Malayalam).
Step 2:
Applicant Selection
Eligible applicants shall be selected by seniority in age on a first-come-first-served basis.
In cases where more than 50 applications are received in a year, applicants may be selected by drawing lots, which must be done in the presence of any three applicants.
Disbursement of Benefits
Step 1:
Applying for Central Subsidy
After obtaining the licence, the applicant may submit an application under the Pradhan Mantri E-DRIVE (FAME-3) Scheme to avail a maximum subsidy of ₹50,000/-.
Step 2:
Final Disbursement of State Subsidy
The balance subsidy amount will be paid according to the purchase status.
If the beneficiary already bought the vehicle, the subsidy is credited to their account upon submission of the original invoice.

If the vehicle has not been purchased, the applicant must pay 25% of the total cost to the dealer first, and the balance subsidy amount will then be credited directly to the authorized dealer’s account.
Monitoring and Compliance
The beneficiary list shall be uploaded in the Urban Development Housing portal to avoid duplication of benefits covered by schemes being implemented by other Departments.
The Registering Authority shall block the transaction for transfer of ownership in the integrated software used by the Transport Department (VAHAN) for a period of 5 years.

applicationProcess.process_md: **Step 1:** The interested applicant should take a printout of the [prescribed format of the application form](https://styandptg.py.gov.in/2025/JULY/EXTRAORDINARYPART-II/1/60-PART-II%20dated%2031-07-2025.pdf). **Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). **Step 3:** The applicants in the Puducherry region should submit the application to the [Director](https://adwelfare.py.gov.in/contacts). The applicants in the Karaikal and Yanam regions should submit the application to the [Assistant Director](https://adwelfare.py.gov.in/contacts). The applicants in the Mahe region should submit the application to the [Regional Administrator](https://adwelfare.py.gov.in/contacts). ##### ***Post-Application Processes*** **Step 1:** **Review and Selection Methodology** - All applications must be reviewed by the designated Welfare Inspector, and only those applications deemed eligible will be considered by the Selection Committee. - Selection should be made by giving an advertisement in the leading dailies (one English and one regional language daily, such as Tamil, Telugu, or Malayalam). **Step 2:** **Applicant Selection** - Eligible applicants shall be selected by seniority in age on a first-come-first-served basis. - In cases where more than 50 applications are received in a year, applicants may be selected by drawing lots, which must be done in the presence of any three applicants. ##### ***Disbursement of Benefits*** **Step 1:** **Applying for Central Subsidy** After obtaining the licence, the applicant may submit an application under the Pradhan Mantri E-DRIVE (FAME-3) Scheme to avail a maximum subsidy of ₹50,000/-. **Step 2:** **Final Disbursement of State Subsidy** - The balance subsidy amount will be paid according to the purchase status. - If the beneficiary already bought the vehicle, the subsidy is credited to their account upon submission of the original invoice. - If the vehicle has not been purchased, the applicant must pay 25% of the total cost to the dealer first, and the balance subsidy amount will then be credited directly to the authorized dealer’s account. ##### ***Monitoring and Compliance*** - The beneficiary list shall be uploaded in the Urban Development Housing portal to avoid duplication of benefits covered by schemes being implemented by other Departments. - The Registering Authority shall block the transaction for transfer of ownership in the integrated software used by the Transport Department (VAHAN) for a period of 5 years.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: - The applicant should belong to the Union Territory of Puducherry either by virtue of birth or by virtue of continuous residence of not less than 5 years. - The applicant must be of the Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe community recognized by the Union Territory of Puducherry. - The applicant must be a woman. - The total annual income of the applicant and her spouse must not exceed ₹2,00,000/-. - The applicant must be at least 18 years old on the date of application. - The applicant must not be more than 45 years old on the date of application. - The applicant must not be availing assistance under a prolonged disease. - The applicant must have a valid driving licence for operating the electric auto issued by a Competent Authority. - The applicant must not have availed of a similar subsidy under any other State / Central Scheme for the same purpose. - Only one person per family can avail the benefit once in their lifetime. _*Applicants shall be selected by seniority in age on a first-come-first-served basis._

children.text: The applicant should belong to the Union Territory of Puducherry either by virtue of birth or by virtue of continuous residence of not less than 5 years.
The applicant must be of the Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe community recognized by the Union Territory of Puducherry.
The applicant must be a woman.
The total annual income of the applicant and her spouse must not exceed ₹2,00,000/-.
The applicant must be at least 18 years old on the date of application.
The applicant must not be more than 45 years old on the date of application.
The applicant must not be availing assistance under a prolonged disease.
The applicant must have a valid driving licence for operating the electric auto issued by a Competent Authority.
The applicant must not have availed of a similar subsidy under any other State / Central Scheme for the same purpose.
Only one person per family can avail the benefit once in their lifetime.
*Applicants shall be selected by seniority in age on a first-come-first-served basis.
